channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he's joining tariff talks with Japan as US seeks deals amid trade wars
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday inserted himself directly into trade talks with Japanese officials, a sign of the high stakes for the United States after its tariffs rattled the economy and caused the administration to assure the public that it would quickly reach deals.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, MARI YAMAGUCHI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds probable cause to hold Trump administration in contempt for violating deportation order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday said he has found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt of court for violating his orders last month to turn around planes carrying deportees to an El Salvador prison. The ruling from U.S District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful House leadership chair, Stefanik poised to take on Hochul for NY governor
item.description: New York Rep. Elise Stefanik is considering a bid for governor in 2026, multiple sources close to her confirmed to Fox News Digital following a Wall Street Journal report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Third top Pentagon aide Collin Carroll on administrative leave over leaks probe
item.description: Colin Carroll became the third top Pentagon official sidelined in two days as President Donald Trump's Defense Department cracks down on leaks to reporters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP Rep. Elise Stefanik is considering a run for governor of New York
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik, a member of House Republican leadership and onetime nominee for U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, is considering a run for New York governor, according to a person close to her.
item.dc:creator: Seung Min Kim And Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration plans to end the IRS Direct File program for free tax filing, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration plans to eliminate the IRS' Direct File program, an electronic system for filing tax returns directly to the agency for free, according to two people familiar with the decision.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance to visit leaders of Italy, India
item.description: Vice President Vance will travel next week to Italy and India to meet with leaders there and visit cultural sites. Vance will be joined by second lady Usha Vance and their children for the trip, which will begin Friday with a trip to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge: 'Probable cause' to hold U.S. in contempt over Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The government sent several planeloads of alleged gang members to El Salvador, including 137 people under the act, the White House said at the time. The judge gave the U.S. until April 23 to respond.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: US imposes sanctions on a Chinese refinery accused of buying Iranian oil
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department imposed sanctions Wednesday on a Chinese refinery accused of purchasing more than $1 billion worth of Iranian oil, saying the proceeds help finance both Tehran's government and Iran's support for militant groups.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds probable cause to hold Trump administration in contempt for violating deportation order
item.description: A federal judge says he's found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt of court for violating his orders last month to turn around planes carrying deportees to an El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: By Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kentucky senate race for McConnell’s seat heats up
item.description: Kentucky Rep. Andy Barr posted millions in first quarter fundraising Tuesday as the senate race for the soon vacant seat of Sen. Mitch McConnell continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside House Republicans' new task force to battle criminal Mexican cartels
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw spoke with Fox News Digital about his new task force to combat Mexican drug cartels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan senators push tougher penalties for foreign-backed crimes on US soil
item.description: Sens. Joni Ernst, Elissa Slotkin, Jim Banks and Maggie Hassan introduced the DETERRENCE Act to crack down on plots targeting U.S. citizens taken up by foreign governments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republican DOGE leader Joni Ernst faces first Democratic challenger in 2026 race
item.description: Senate DOGE leader Joni Ernst, R-Iowa, is facing a Democratic challenge from Nathan Sage, a Marine and Army veteran running in the 2026 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EPA can't end grants from $20 billion Biden-era fund for climate-friendly projects, a judge says
item.description: A federal judge says some nonprofits awarded billions for a so-called green bank to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects cannot have their contracts scrapped and must have access to some of the frozen money.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bernie Sanders, AOC and other anti-Trump progressives haul in big bucks for 2026 midterms
item.description: Progressive Capitol Hill lawmakers who are among the most vocal in pushing back against President Donald Trump's aggressive second-term agenda are seeing a surge in fundraising.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Judge finds probable cause to hold administration in contempt over deportation order
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday said he has found probable cause to hold President Donald Trump’s administration in criminal contempt of court for violating his orders to turn around planes carrying deportees to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major university medical center accused of hiding DEI programs, influential Senator calls them out
item.description: After Vanderbilt University Medical Center was allegedly caught hiding their DEI resources, Tennessee GOP Sen. Marsha Blackburn is calling on the medical school to comply with President Trump's orders demanding they such programs be halted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Lawyers for Harvard in Trump administration dispute are no strangers to high-profile legal matters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The two attorneys representing Harvard University in a pitched fight with the Trump administration are no strangers to the spotlight or to Washington investigations that reach into the White House.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Army and Air Force libraries are ordered to review books for DEI material
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Army and Air Force libraries have been told to go through their stacks to find books related to diversity, equity and inclusion, according to new memos obtained by The Associated Press. The orders from service leaders come about two weeks after the U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon senior adviser Dan Caldwell ousted during investigation into leaks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Caldwell, a senior adviser to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, has been placed on administrative leave and was escorted out of the Pentagon by security on Tuesday, two defense officials said.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump assassination plot part of domestic terrorism rise: Ex-FBI
item.description: Ex-FBI special agent Stuart Kaplan joined "NewsNation Now" to discuss how it is increasing across the United States, largely thanks to social media.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: 281 bills fall victim to deadline at Nevada Legislature. Here are the highlights
item.description: A total of 281 bills missed a Friday deadline to win committee passage in Carson City, the first major deadline at the Nevada Legislature this session. If that seems like a lot, consider that 875 bills are still alive.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Illinois Democrats mull next moves after strong showing in April elections
item.description: Gov. JB Pritzker and top state Democrats gathered Monday at a union hall in DuPage County to celebrate the party's near clean sweep of Illinois municipal elections two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence. It began gaining momentum after the Supreme Court’s 2022 ruling overturning Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News
